codeReview.summary: Clean, well-structured implementation of configurable display resolution via a two-phase GPU driver protocol. The hardcoded FB_WIDTH/FB_HEIGHT/FB_STRIDE/FB_SIZE constants are fully removed from init.rs. The GPU driver now queries GET_DISPLAY_INFO, reports dimensions back to init via MSG_DISPLAY_INFO, and init allocates framebuffers dynamically at the correct size. Kernel limits (MAX_ORDER, MAX_DMA_ORDER, memory_share page limit) are consistently updated across all three files. The zero-dimension fallback (1024x768) is handled correctly. The protocol respects the handle_send constraint on unstarted processes by creating and sending channel endpoints before process_start. Tests are updated to match new limits. No bugs found.

issues.description: Stale module docstring: says 'up to order MAX_ORDER = 4 MiB' but MAX_ORDER is now 11 (8 MiB). The constant itself is correct; only the comment is outdated.

No upper-bound validation on queried display dimensions. If virtio-gpu reports an absurdly large resolution (e.g. 16000x16000), the dma_alloc in init will fail with an error message and exit, so there's no UB — but there's no graceful fallback to a safe resolution. Acceptable for this prototype stage.

GpuConfig message in phase 4 still populates mmio_pa and irq fields that the GPU driver already received via the DeviceConfig message in phase 2. These fields are redundant — the GPU driver only reads fb_pa, fb_pa2, and fb_size from the second message. Harmless (fits in 60-byte payload) but could be cleaned up to a smaller struct.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted in skillFeedback that the os-worker skill should mention the handle_send constraint on unstarted processes. This constraint is critical for protocol design — the worker had to design the two-phase protocol around it (creating compositor→GPU channel and sending endpoint B before process_start). The skill procedure says 'When modifying init's process setup, ensure handle numbering is consistent' but doesn't mention the unstarted-process requirement for handle_send.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The os-worker skill could mention that handle_send only works on unstarted processes — this is a critical constraint that affects protocol design when processes need to communicate bidirectionally during startup.' The worker correctly worked around this constraint in the implementation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Module-level docstrings that reference specific constant values (like MAX_ORDER's byte limit) are not consistently updated when the constant changes. The page_allocator.rs docstring still says '4 MiB' after MAX_ORDER was changed from 10 to 11 (8 MiB). The worker updated the boot log string in main.rs and the comment next to the constant, but missed the module-level docstring.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/kernel/page_allocator.rs:5 — '//! order MAX_ORDER = 4 MiB)' should read '8 MiB'. The constant comment on line 17 was correctly updated to '/// Maximum order: 2^11 pages = 8 MiB.'

summary: PASS. The configurable-resolution feature is well-implemented with a clean two-phase GPU driver protocol. All hardcoded framebuffer constants are removed. The implementation correctly queries GET_DISPLAY_INFO, handles the zero-dimension edge case with a 1024x768 fallback, dynamically allocates framebuffers, and updates all kernel limits consistently (MAX_ORDER 10→11, MAX_DMA_ORDER 10→11, memory_share 1024→2048). Tests are updated. Three non-blocking issues noted: stale docstring in page_allocator.rs, no upper-bound on queried resolution (graceful exit on alloc failure is present), and redundant fields in the phase-4 GpuConfig message. Worker followed procedure correctly and verified at 1024x768, 1280x800, and 1920x1080.
